title: Gravimetric Water Content (GWC)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"96ahe","text":"The method is the mass of water per mass of dry soil. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"4uaqk","text":"Small weighing tins (3 per site and depth)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fmu5e","text":"Soil (~  per site and depth)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":7,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"8ldo","text":"Balance","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"51ku5","text":"Drying oven set to   or   ","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":19,"length":1},{"key":2,"offset":24,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"e8sfn","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"amount","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"amount":"30","error":false,"guid":"CBC5B120306E11ED81F0E3DE69CB6030","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"unit":6}},"1":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"D3425930306E11ED81F0E3DE69CB6030","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"60","unit":10}},"2":{"type":"temperature","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"error_temperature":false,"guid":"D8A262D0306E11ED81F0E3DE69CB6030","id":0,"label":"","scientificNotation":false,"temperature":"100","unit":10}}}}
steps: Record the mass of the empty labeled tin in the Core processing file. Add 10 g, soil and record the mass of the tin and soil (this should be completed in the core processing step.
Place the soils in the drying oven for 24:00:00 at 100 °C .
After 24:00:00 remove samples from drying oven and allow to cool. Record the mass of the soil and tin.
Return samples to the drying oven for an additional 24:00:00 .
Repeat step 3 and record the new mass. If the mass is still changing repeat steps 3 and 4 until no further change.
